SAYS an. p" WiTTER

pages.raw_text:
A Northern Pacific engine backed
into another in the local yards a
few days ago. There happened to
be an employee of the railroad COM
pany between the two when the
shock came. He Is dead. The
chronicle of the event was given
the people by the morning paper
under the headline, Engine Kills
Walter Rhea."


The closing paragraph of the mar
rative was as follows: Coroner
Witter said last night that he did
not consider an inquest necessary.
People who saw the accident de
clared it to be accidental and that
no blame could be attached to the
railroad company"


Who saw the accident except en-
ployes of the corporation? Who de-
termined that the accident" was
accidental" but other employees of
the railroad company? Who is the
coroner who did not consider an
inquest necessary" but surgeon
in the employ of the company?


The democratic platform says:
I'VE favor the adoption by the leg
islature of law relating to in
juries to an employee, similar to the
law enacted by the last congress
relating to personal injuries


Here again the democratic party
goes on record in the interests OF
the man who tolls in dangerous po-
sitions. Some fellow employee was
responsible for the death of Walter
Rhea. 1t was the hand of some man
who bumped one engine into an
other. and created the situation
where an inquest was net neces-
sary and no blame could be at
tached to the railroad company.


Corporation greed, backed up and
made to appear plausible by hired
minions, Is one of the menaces OF
the time. In its alliance with the
republican candidates in this coun-
ty there Is no moderation. The only
way in which this slavery can be
broken Is by the ballot. The only
protection any citizen can have
against this overpowering union of
power and plumber is to turn both
out of public places. This. the dem
ocracy Is trying to do. ard In its
work asks your aid.
